"filed": best["filed"],
    }


def get_sbc(ticker: str, periods: int = 4) -> dict:
    """Return up to `periods` years of stock-based compensation from XBRL facts.

    Reads the SBC line straight from the companyfacts cash-flow / income facts,
    covering both US filers (10-K, us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensation) and foreign
    private issuers (20-F, ifrs-full:ExpenseFromSharebasedPaymentTransactions-
    WithEmployees). This is the fallback /stock-signal uses when yfinance returns
    null SBC — common for non-US filers whose 20-F cash-flow line Yahoo doesn't
    normalise.

    SBC is a flow (duration) fact, so the same fiscal year appears once per
    filing that restates it; entries are deduplicated by period-end, keeping the
    most recently filed value.

    Returns:
        {
            "ticker": str,
            "filing_type": str,       # "10-K" or "20-F" — the form the values came from
            "currency": str,          # reporting currency, e.g. "USD" or "KZT"
            "periods": [              # newest first, up to `periods` entries
                {"fiscal_year": "YYYY-MM-DD", "sbc": int, "source": "edgar_xbrl"},
                ...
            ],
        }

    Raises:
        EDGARNoDataError: ticker unknown, or no SBC concept present in any annual
            filing — the caller should treat this as "SBC line not present in
            filing" (some issuers genuinely report $0 / no SBC).
    """
    if periods < 1:
        raise ValueError(f"periods must be >= 1, got {periods}")

    cik = _get_cik(ticker)
    url = f"{EDGAR_BASE}/api/xbrl/companyfacts/CIK{cik}.json"
    resp = requests.get(url, headers=_HEADERS)
    resp.raise_for_status()
    facts = resp.json().get("facts", {})

    for namespace, concept in _SBC_CONCEPTS:
        concept_data = facts.get(namespace, {}).get(concept)
        if not concept_data:
            continue

        units = concept_data.get("units", {})
        # Prefer USD; otherwise take the single reporting currency the filer used.
        unit_key = "USD" if "USD" in units else next(iter(units), None)
        if unit_key is None:
            continue

        annual = [
            e for e in units[unit_key] if e.get("form") in _ANNUAL_FORMS_FPI
        ]
        if not annual:
            continue

        # Dedupe by period-end; keep the most recently filed value on collision.
        by_end: dict[str, dict] = {}
        for e in annual:
            end = e["end"]
            if end not in by_end or e["filed"] > by_end[end]["filed"]:
                by_end[end] = e

        selected = sorted(by_end.values(), key=lambda e: e["end"], reverse=True)[
            :periods
        ]
        filing_type = selected[0]["form"]

        return {
            "ticker": ticker,
            "filing_type": filing_type,
            "currency": unit_key,
            "periods": [
                {
                    "fiscal_year": e["end"],
                    "sbc": e["val"],
                    "source": "edgar_xbrl",
                }
                for e in selected
            ],
        }

    raise EDGARNoDataError(
        f"no stock-based-compensation concept present in any annual filing for "
        f"{ticker}; tried {', '.join(f'{ns}:{c}' for ns, c in _SBC_CONCEPTS)}. "
        f"SBC line not present in filing (issuer may genuinely have $0 SBC)."
    )

content.subject: Implement get_sbc in EDGAR tools.py
content.description: Add get_sbc(ticker, periods=4) to mcp/edgar/tools.py. Searches us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensation and ifrs-full:ExpenseFromSharebasedPaymentTransactionsWithEmployees. Handles 10-K/10-K/A/20-F/20-F/A forms, dedupes by period-end (latest filed wins), returns {ticker, filing_type, currency, periods:[{fiscal_year, sbc, source:edgar_xbrl}]}. Raises EDGARNoDataError with explicit 'SBC line not present' when no candidate concept has annual data.

content.text: Now I'll refactor the unit selection to be deterministic and make the annual-entry filter gate namespace selection (so a us-gaap concept with no annual entries falls through to IFRS), plus document the us-gaap-first intent and the semantic-proxy caveat:

input.old_string:
Reads the SBC line straight from the companyfacts cash-flow / income facts,
    covering both US filers (10-K, us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensation) and foreign
    private issuers (20-F, ifrs-full:ExpenseFromSharebasedPaymentTransactions-
    WithEmployees). This is the fallback /stock-signal uses when yfinance returns
    null SBC — common for non-US filers whose 20-F cash-flow line Yahoo doesn't
    normalise.

    SBC is a flow (duration) fact, so the same fiscal year appears once per
    filing that restates it; entries are deduplicated by period-end, keeping the
    most recently filed value.
input.new_string:
Reads the SBC line straight from the companyfacts cash-flow / income facts,
    covering both US filers (10-K, us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensation) and foreign
    private issuers (20-F, ifrs-full:ExpenseFromSharebasedPaymentTransactions-
    WithEmployees). This is the fallback /stock-signal uses when yfinance returns
    null SBC — common for non-US filers whose 20-F cash-flow line Yahoo doesn't
    normalise.

    The two concepts are close but not definitionally identical: the us-gaap one
    is the cash-flow-statement non-cash add-back; the ifrs-full one is the
    income-statement employee share-based-payment expense (which excludes
    non-employee/supplier awards and any capitalised amounts). For EPS stripping
    they are a sound proxy for each other, but they are not a like-for-like
    substitute — treat the ifrs-full figure as an approximation of the cash-flow
    SBC, not an exact equal.

    Candidates are tried us-gaap first, then ifrs-full: the us-gaap cash-flow
    figure is the closest match to the yfinance value being replaced, so a filer
    that tags both resolves to us-gaap. A namespace whose concept carries no
    annual-form entries (only quarterly) is skipped, so an FPI lacking real
    us-gaap annual data falls through to ifrs-full.

    SBC is a flow (duration) fact, so the same fiscal year appears once per
    filing that restates it; entries are deduplicated by period-end, keeping the
    most recently filed value.

attachment.snippet:
1	# Linear workflow — initiative model
2	
3	This document is the canonical governance model for how work is tracked in Linear across all repos in this workspace. It applies to: agent-skills-shaper, stock-review, agent-skills, nestl, adyen-onboarding.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## The initiative model
8	
9	An **initiative** is a time-bounded, goal-oriented body of work with a stated success criterion and a bounded appetite. It is not a repo alias, not a backlog, and not a feature list.
10	
11	An initiative is ready to enter a cycle when it can answer all six of these fields:
12	
13	```
14	Goal:           For [who], we want to [solve problem / achieve outcome].
15	Key results:    1. [committed|aspirational] — [observable state — binary pass/fail,
16	                      fitness function firing, or measurable delta]
17	                   baseline: [current value/state]
18	                   target:   [value/state we expect at the end]
19	                   window:   [when this is judged — "by end of cycle", "within 30 days"]
20	                   source:   [where the evidence lives — file path, log, Linear query]
21	                2. [committed|aspirational] — [observable state]
22	                   baseline: ...  target: ...  window: ...  source: ...
23	                3. [committed|aspirational] — [observable state]
24	                   baseline: ...  target: ...  window: ...  source: ...
25	                (3–5 KRs total; each verifiable by inspecting the system —
26	                 no "improve X" / "better Y" / "run N times" language)
27	Affected repos: [list]
28	Appetite:       ~[N] issues  (not days or weeks)
29	Kill condition: [observable state that says "the bet didn't work, walk away"]
30	Project type:   [1: methodology | 2: personal product | 3: utility skill pack |
31	                 4: research/thesis | 5: equity research | 6: production]
32	```
33	
34	The format is OKR-shaped: the Goal is the Objective (qualitative, what we want to achieve), and Key results are the 3–5 observable states that must be true for the initiative to be Done. KRs are written so a future agent (or you on a fresh session) can verify each by looking at the system — no "improve X" / "better Y" language. Each KR carries four sub-fields (baseline / target / window / source) and a `[committed|aspirational]` tag that sets the grading bar at cycle close.
35	
36	If any of the six fields can't be filled, the initiative is not ready. Create it as a Draft in Linear but don't assign it to a cycle.
37	
38	### Initiative size
39	
40	| Size | Issue count | Notes |
41	|---|---|---|
42	| Too small | < 5 | Not an initiative — create a standalone issue or put it in the ops slot |
43	| Small | 5–8 | One cycle slot with room left for other initiatives |
44	| Medium | 9–12 | One full cycle slot |
45	| Large | 13–15 | Full cycle slot; very little room for anything else |
46	| Too large | > 15 | Split into two initiatives before committing |
47	
48	### Initiative lifecycle
49	
50	| State | Meaning |
51	|---|---|
52	| **Draft** | Idea exists; goal or criterion not yet written |
53	| **Ready** | All six fields confirmed — Goal + Key results (with sub-fields + tags) + Affected repos + Appetite + Kill condition + Project type; can enter a cycle |
54	| **Active** | Assigned to the current cycle; work in progress |
55	| **Done** | Success criterion observed (or definitively ruled out) — not just issues closed |
56	| **Paused** | Deprioritised mid-cycle; carries over with a note on why |
57	| **Canceled** | Kill condition triggered, or initiative definitively ruled out mid-cycle; one-sentence reason recorded |
58	
59	**Done ≠ all issues closed.** An initiative closes when the key results are observed (or definitively ruled out) — not when its issue list reaches zero. An initiative that shipped everything but the KRs didn't hold is not Done; it is Paused for a retrospective.
60	
61	### Creating an initiative
62	
63	Use the `/initiative-shape` skill. Do not create Linear projects by hand for goal-directed work — the skill enforces the six-field check (including the 11-rule verification rubric gate at Step 6.5) before creating the project.
64	
65	Direct creation is permitted only for: maintenance buckets, ops slots, and one-off standalone issue groupings.
66	
67	---
68	
69	## Cycle model
70	
71	A cycle is 3–4 working days of focused work plus 1 planning day.
72	
73	### Cycle composition
74	
75	Every cycle has **exactly four slots**:
76	
77	| Slot | Type | Goal/criterion required? |
78	|---|---|---|
79	| Initiative 1 | Goal-oriented | Yes |
80	| Initiative 2 | Goal-oriented | Yes |
81	| Initiative 3 | Goal-oriented | Yes |
82	| Ops slot | Maintenance | No |
83	
84	The ops slot is not an initiative. It exists for: bug fixes, compliance items, emergent issues, one-offs, and KTLO work. Ops slot issues either have no project assigned, or live in the team's **ops container project** — a perpetual Backlog-state project named e.g. "Ops — bugs, maintenance, emergent" that holds ops work. The container project carries no Goal and no Key Results because it is not an initiative — it exists only because MCP tooling can't clear an issue's project assignment, so issues created via Claude Code need a non-initiative home.
85	
86	**Do not add a 4th initiative.** The ops slot is not a buffer for overflow from the three initiative slots; it is a deliberate reservation for non-initiative work that would otherwise eat into initiative time unplanned. The ops container project is not an initiative either — it doesn't get a Goal, KRs, or appetite, and it never enters the Done state.
87	
88	### Cycle planning
89	
90	On planning day:
91	1. Confirm 3 initiatives are in Ready state (six-field check passes for each: Goal + Key results with sub-fields + Affected repos + Appetite + Kill condition + Project type).
92	2. Identify the ops slot: pull 2–5 issues from the team backlog (bugs, maintenance, one-offs) into the cycle as standalone issues.
93	3. For each initiative, confirm which issues in its backlog will be worked this cycle. Do not try to clear the entire initiative backlog in one cycle — prioritise by what moves a Key Result.
94	4. Assign all confirmed issues to the cycle.
95	
96	### Cycle close
97	
98	At cycle end, for each initiative:
99	- If all key results hold (or were definitively ruled out): mark initiative Done. Write one sentence in the Linear project description noting what was observed.
100	- If the work shipped but KRs didn't hold yet: note this; either carry the initiative into the next cycle (Active) or pause it for a retrospective.
101	- If the initiative is being killed: mark Cancelled with a one-sentence reason. This is a normal outcome, not a failure.
102	
103	---
104	
105	## Backlog
106	
107	**Backlog = team issues with no project assigned, plus issues in the ops container project.**
108	
109	Issues enter the backlog when:
110	- They don't belong to any current initiative
111	- They surface mid-flight as non-initiative work (bugs, one-offs)
112	- An initiative is killed and its remaining issues are descoped
113	
114	Issues leave the backlog at cycle planning: either assigned to an initiative project, pulled into the ops slot for the current cycle, or explicitly deferred to a future cycle.
115	
116	The backlog is not the idea bank. The idea bank (from `idea-triage`) holds unvalidated product hypotheses. The backlog holds concrete issues that are ready to be worked but not yet assigned.
117	
118	---
119	
120	## Issue workflow
121	
122	### On start
123	
124	- Move to **In Progress** via `mcp__claude_ai_Linear__save_issue`.
125	- If the issue isn't yet in the current cycle and you intend to ship it this cycle, assign it to the current cycle.
126	- Every issue must be either (a) assigned to an initiative project, or (b) explicitly in the ops slot — meaning either no project assigned, or in the ops container project. An issue with neither an initiative nor an ops home is untracked — don't let this happen.

def test_rejects_non_positive_periods(self):
        with patch("tools.requests.get", side_effect=_mock_get_sbc):
            with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="periods"):
                get_sbc("NVDA", 0)
input.new_string:
def test_rejects_non_positive_periods(self):
        with patch("tools.requests.get", side_effect=_mock_get_sbc):
            with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="periods"):
                get_sbc("NVDA", 0)

    def test_dual_tagged_filer_prefers_us_gaap(self):
        """When both namespaces carry annual SBC, us-gaap wins (closest to yfinance)."""
        with patch("tools.requests.get", side_effect=_mock_get_sbc):
            result = get_sbc("DUAL", 4)
        assert result["currency"] == "USD"
        assert result["filing_type"] == "10-K"
        assert result["periods"][0]["sbc"] == 500_000_000

    def test_falls_through_when_us_gaap_has_only_quarterly(self):
        """A us-gaap concept with no annual entries must not shadow ifrs-full."""
        with patch("tools.requests.get", side_effect=_mock_get_sbc):
            result = get_sbc("QONLY", 4)
        assert result["currency"] == "GBP"
        assert result["filing_type"] == "20-F"
        assert result["periods"][0]["sbc"] == 88_000_000

    def test_non_usd_unit_selection_is_deterministic(self):
        """With no USD and multiple currencies, the one with most annual entries wins."""
        with patch("tools.requests.get", side_effect=_mock_get_sbc):
            result = get_sbc("MULTICCY", 4)
        assert result["currency"] == "CHF"
        assert len(result["periods"]) == 2
        assert result["periods"][0]["sbc"] == 11_000_000

    def test_periods_larger_than_available_returns_all(self):
        with patch("tools.requests.get", side_effect=_mock_get_sbc):
            result = get_sbc("KSPI", 99)
        assert len(result["periods"]) == 4

--- Changes ---

mcp/edgar/tools.py
  @@ -10,6 +10,20 @@ _TICKERS_URL = "https://www.sec.gov/files/company_tickers.json"
  +# Annual forms including the 20-F foreign private issuer report. Used only by
  +# get_sbc — the other tools stay 10-K-only to preserve their existing behaviour.
  +_ANNUAL_FORMS_FPI = _ANNUAL_FORMS | {"20-F", "20-F/A"}
  +
  +# SBC concept candidates, tried in order. US filers tag the cash-flow add-back
  +# under us-gaap; IFRS filers (20-F foreign private issuers like KSPI) tag the
  +# employee share-based payment expense under ifrs-full. Only concepts verified
  +# against live companyfacts are listed — adding unverified element names risks
  +# silently matching the wrong fact.
  +_SBC_CONCEPTS = [
  +    ("us-gaap", "ShareBasedCompensation"),
  +    ("ifrs-full", "ExpenseFromSharebasedPaymentTransactionsWithEmployees"),
  +]
  +
   
   class EDGARNoDataError(Exception):
       """EDGAR returned no usable data for this ticker or concept."""
  @@ -113,6 +127,119 @@ def get_filing_facts(ticker: str, concept: str) -> dict:
  +def get_sbc(ticker: str, periods: int = 4) -> dict:
  +    """Return up to `periods` years of stock-based compensation from XBRL facts.
  +
  +    Reads the SBC line straight from the companyfacts cash-flow / income facts,
  +    covering both US filers (10-K, us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensation) and foreign
  +    private issuers (20-F, ifrs-full:ExpenseFromSharebasedPaymentTransactions-
  +    WithEmployees). This is the fallback /stock-signal uses when yfinance returns
  +    null SBC — common for non-US filers whose 20-F cash-flow line Yahoo doesn't
  +    normalise.
  +
  +    The two concepts are close but not definitionally identical: the us-gaap one
  +    is the cash-flow-statement non-cash add-back; the ifrs-full one is the
  +    income-statement employee share-based-payment expense (which excludes
  +    non-employee/supplier awards and any capitalised amounts). For EPS stripping
  +    they are a sound proxy for each other, but they are not a like-for-like
  +    substitute — treat the ifrs-full figure as an approximation of the cash-flow
  +    SBC, not an exact equal.
  +
  +    Candidates are tried us-gaap first, then ifrs-full: the us-gaap cash-flow
  +    figure is the closest match to the yfinance value being replaced, so a filer
  +    that tags both resolves to us-gaap. A namespace whose concept carries no
  +    annual-form entries (only quarterly) is skipped, so an FPI lacking real
  +    us-gaap annual data falls through to ifrs-full.
  +
  +    SBC is a flow (duration) fact, so the same fiscal year appears once per
  +    filing that restates it; entries are deduplicated by period-end, keeping the
  +    most recently filed value.
  +
  +    Returns:
  +        {
  +            "ticker": str,
  +            "filing_type": str,       # "10-K" or "20-F" — the form the values came from
  +            "currency": str,          # reporting currency, e.g. "USD" or "KZT"
  +            "periods": [              # newest first, up to `periods` entries
  +                {"fiscal_year": "YYYY-MM-DD", "sbc": int, "source": "edgar_xbrl"},
  +                ...
  +            ],
  +        }
  +
  +    Raises:
  +        EDGARNoDataError: ticker unknown, or no SBC concept present in any annual
  +            filing — the caller should treat this as "SBC line not present in
  +            filing" (some issuers genuinely report $0 / no SBC).
  +    """
  +    if periods < 1:
  +        raise ValueError(f"periods must be >= 1, got {periods}")
  +
  +    cik = _get_cik(ticker)
  +    url = f"{EDGAR_BASE}/api/xbrl/companyfacts/CIK{cik}.json"
  +    resp = requests.get(url, headers=_HEADERS)
  +    resp.raise_for_status()
  +    facts = resp.json().get("facts", {})
  +
  +    for namespace, concept in _SBC_CONCEPTS:
  +        concept_data = facts.get(namespace, {}).get(concept)
  +        if not concept_data:
  +            continue
  +
  +        units = concept_data.get("units", {})
  +        # Group annual-form entries by reporting currency, dropping currencies
  +        # with no annual data.
  +        annual_by_unit = {
  +            unit: annual
  +            for unit, entries in units.items()
  +            if (annual := [e for e in entries if e.get("form") in _ANNUAL_FORMS_FPI])
  +        }
  +        if not annual_by_unit:
  +            continue
  +
  +        # Prefer USD; otherwise pick deterministically — the currency with the
  +        # most annual entries (the filer's primary reporting currency), breaking
  +        # ties on the currency code so the result never depends on dict ordering.
  +        if "USD" in annual_by_unit:
  +            unit_key = "USD"
  +        else:
  +            unit_key = sorted(
  +                annual_by_unit, key=lambda u: (-len(annual_by_unit[u]), u)
  +            )[0]
  +        annual = annual_by_unit[unit_key]
  +
  +        # Dedupe by period-end; keep the most recently filed value on collision.
  +        by_end: dict[str, dict] = {}
  +        for e in annual:
  +            end = e["end"]
  +            if end not in by_end or e["filed"] > by_end[end]["filed"]:
  +                by_end[end] = e
  +
  +        selected = sorted(by_end.values(), key=lambda e: e["end"], reverse=True)[
  +            :periods
  +        ]
  +        filing_type = selected[0]["form"]
  +
  +        return {
  +            "ticker": ticker,
  +            "filing_type": filing_type,
  +            "currency": unit_key,
  +            "periods": [
  +                {
  +                    "fiscal_year": e["end"],
  +                    "sbc": e["val"],
  ... (13 lines truncated)
  +127 -0

content.subject: Implement get_sbc in EDGAR tools.py
content.description: Add get_sbc(ticker, periods=4) to mcp/edgar/tools.py. Searches us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensation and ifrs-full:ExpenseFromSharebasedPaymentTransactionsWithEmployees. Handles 10-K/10-K/A/20-F/20-F/A forms, dedupes by period-end (latest filed wins), returns {ticker, filing_type, currency, periods:[{fiscal_year, sbc, source:edgar_xbrl}]}. Raises EDGARNoDataError with explicit 'SBC line not present' when no candidate concept has annual data.

content.description: Add @mcp.tool() wrapper for get_sbc in mcp/edgar/server.py with a clear docstring.

content.subject: Write get_sbc tests

content.subject: Wire EDGAR SBC fallback into /stock-signal
content.description: Update skills/stock-signal/SKILL.md: when yfinance SBC null, call EDGAR get_sbc before manual-input/N-A; currency reconciliation via get_fx_rate; tag source:edgar in SIGNAL OUTPUT.

git add .mcp.json DESIGN.md mcp/edgar/server.py mcp/edgar/test_tools.py mcp/edgar/tools.py skills/stock-signal/SKILL.md && git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
feat(edgar): get_sbc XBRL fallback for null yfinance SBC (ABA-75)

Adds get_sbc(ticker, periods=4) to the EDGAR MCP, reading multi-year
stock-based compensation straight from companyfacts XBRL. Covers US
filers (us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensation, 10-K, USD) and foreign private
issuers (ifrs-full:ExpenseFromSharebasedPaymentTransactionsWithEmployees,
20-F — e.g. KSPI in KZT), which yfinance returns as null. Dedupes by
period-end (latest filed wins), prefers us-gaap when both namespaces are
tagged, and raises an explicit "SBC line not present" error rather than
assuming $0.

Registers the previously-unwired EDGAR server in .mcp.json so the tool is
callable at runtime, and wires it into /stock-signal as the SBC fallback
before the manual-input protocol — with mandatory currency reconciliation
(get_fx_rate, explicit multiply + magnitude sanity-check) and an
sbc_source=edgar tag that caps confidence at MEDIUM.

Verified live: get_sbc("KSPI",4) returns 4yr KZT from the 20-F facts;
NVDA/CRM/AAPL return USD from 10-K. 46 unit tests pass.
EOF
git --no-pager log --oneline -1

input.body:
Shipped in `ebdf755` (pushed to main).

**What landed**
- New EDGAR tool `get_sbc(ticker, periods=4)` reading multi-year SBC from companyfacts XBRL. US path: `us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensation` (10-K, USD). Foreign-filer path: `ifrs-full:ExpenseFromSharebasedPaymentTransactionsWithEmployees` (20-F, local currency). Dedupes by period-end (latest filed wins), prefers us-gaap when both are tagged, raises explicit "SBC line not present" rather than assuming $0.
- Registered the EDGAR MCP server in `.mcp.json` — it was built under ABA-13/14 but never wired into the runtime config, so `/signal` couldn't actually call it. Without this the fallback would have been inert.
- Wired into `/stock-signal` as the SBC fallback before the manual-input protocol, with mandatory currency reconciliation and an `sbc_source` tag (caps confidence at MEDIUM when `edgar`).

**Done-when verification (live)**
- `get_sbc("KSPI", 4)` → 4yr from 20-F XBRL, KZT: 2025=15.476B, 2024=16.963B, 2023=20.859B, 2022=19.984B ✅
- US 10-K sanity check → NVDA (6.386B USD FY26), CRM, AAPL (12.863B USD FY25) ✅
- `/signal` calls the fallback automatically and tags `source: edgar` ✅

**Code review (sub-agent, 5-axis):** 1 Critical, 3 Required, 3 Nice-to-have.
- Critical (FX direction unspecified — order-of-magnitude risk): **fixed** — SKILL.md now states the `{BASE}{QUOTE}=X` convention, mandates multiply, and includes a magnitude sanity-check.
- Required ×3: nondeterministic non-USD unit pick → **fixed** (deterministic: most annual entries, tie-break on code); dual-namespace ordering → **fixed + tested** (us-gaap-first documented as intentional; a us-gaap concept with only quarterly entries now falls through to ifrs-full); us-gaap vs ifrs semantic non-equivalence → **documented** in docstring + DESIGN.md.
- Nice-to-have: annual-not-TTM now surfaced in the output note (**done**); added dual-namespace / multi-currency / quarterly-fallthrough tests (**done**); shared companyfacts-fetch caching **deferred** (pre-existing across all EDGAR tools — out of scope).

Tests: 46 pass (was 29). Out of scope (per issue): LSE/HKEX-only tickers with no EDGAR presence.

content.text:
{"id":"ABA-75","title":"EDGAR MCP: pull SBC from cash flow statement when yfinance returns null (esp. non-US filers)","description":"## Problem

yfinance returns `stock_based_compensation: null` for some companies — observed on KSPI ([Kaspi.kz](<http://Kaspi.kz>), Nasdaq ADR) across all 4 fiscal years on 2026-05-13. This appears to be more common for non-US filers (20-F filings) where Yahoo's normalisation doesn't reliably surface the SBC line from the cash flow reconciliation.

The /signal skill requires SBC for the Clean EPS calc. Today it falls back to "N/A — SBC not returned by yfinance," which is technically correct but means we can never produce a clean PEG for any company in this category.

## Proposal

Extend the EDGAR MCP (or its planned successor for foreign filers) to pull `StockBasedCompensation` from the XBRL cash-flow facts in 10-K / 20-F / 10-Q filings:

```
get_sbc(ticker: str, periods: int = 4) -> {
  "ticker": "KSPI",
  "filing_type": "20-F",
  "currency": "KZT",
  "periods": [
    {"fiscal_year": "2025-12-31", "sbc": 12500000000, "source": "edgar_xbrl"},
    ...
  ]
}
```

When yfinance returns null SBC, /signal should fall back to this tool before declaring N/A.

## Foreign-filer caveat

Many non-US issuers file 20-F with EDGAR (Kaspi does — they're a Nasdaq-listed FPI). Those will work with the existing EDGAR plumbing planned in <issue id="afcac464-a217-4668-a472-4f62a7c14b54">ABA-13</issue>/14. But truly foreign-listed-only tickers (LSE-only, HKEX-only) will need a separate data source — out of scope here.

## Done when

* `get_sbc("KSPI", 4)` returns 4 years of non-null SBC values pulled from the 20-F XBRL facts (or returns an explicit "SBC line not present in filing" if it's truly absent — some issuers genuinely have $0 SBC).
* /signal calls this fallback automatically when yfinance returns null SBC, and the result is tagged `source: "edgar"` in the SIGNAL OUTPUT block.
* Same for at least one US 10-K filer as a sanity check.

## Dependencies

* <issue id="afcac464-a217-4668-a472-4f62a7c14b54">ABA-13</issue> (EDGAR search_filings)
* <issue id="b62908bf-1194-421b-a0e9-0048a3e62d42">ABA-14</issue> (EDGAR get_filing_facts)

## Related

* <issue id="a35f8c1b-fb10-4077-b7db-89a6309409ac">ABA-72</issue> (KSPI ratios gap)
* Manual-input fallback issue (sibling — this and that issue are different lines of defence; this one is the automated path)","priority":{"value":3,"name":"Medium"},"url":"https://linear.app/ababushkin/issue/ABA-75/edgar-mcp-pull-sbc-from-cash-flow-statement-when-yfinance-returns-null","gitBranchName":"anton/aba-75-edgar-mcp-pull-sbc-from-cash-flow-statement-when-yfinance","createdAt":"2026-05-13T02:09:25.860Z","updatedAt":"2026-05-25T13:26:40.330Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":"2026-05-25T13:26:40.299Z","startedAt":"2026-05-25T13:15:05.660Z","canceledAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Done","statusType":"completed","labels":["repo:stock-screener","model:sonnet"],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Anton Babushkin","createdById":"720d0535-dfc6-465f-8111-d1a3e6eab786","assignee":"Anton Babushkin","assigneeId":"720d0535-dfc6-465f-8111-d1a3e6eab786","project":"Ops - bugs, maintenance, emergent","projectId":"012923d7-25b5-475b-a1a7-5832a54ed389","team":"Personal","teamId":"c20f56da-f121-4653-bf6c-dc1c8aac21c1","cycleId":"3fb7ba8a-ff55-49c0-bf35-b879d3a4b83c"}

input.content:
---
name: edgar-ifrs-namespace-foreign-filers
description: Foreign private issuers (20-F) on EDGAR expose XBRL facts under ifrs-full, not us-gaap — any companyfacts lookup must search both
metadata:
  type: reference
---

SEC EDGAR `companyfacts` exposes foreign private issuers' XBRL facts under the **`ifrs-full`** namespace in their **local reporting currency**, NOT `us-gaap`/USD. A 20-F filer like KSPI (Kaspi.kz, CIK 0001985487) reports SBC as `ifrs-full:ExpenseFromSharebasedPaymentTransactionsWithEmployees` in KZT; its `us-gaap` block has only a couple of stub concepts. Any EDGAR tool that searches only `us-gaap` will silently return nothing for these filers.

Verified 2026-05-25 while building `get_sbc` (ABA-75). The IFRS employee-SBC-expense concept is a close *proxy* for the us-gaap cash-flow add-back, not a definitional equal (it excludes non-employee/capitalised awards).

When extending EDGAR coverage to more concepts for foreign filers (revenue, etc.), expect the same pattern: build a `(namespace, concept)` candidate list spanning both `us-gaap` and `ifrs-full`, handle non-USD units, and include `20-F`/`20-F/A` in the annual-forms set. See `mcp/edgar/tools.py` `get_sbc` for the reference implementation. Out of scope for EDGAR entirely: LSE/HKEX-only tickers (no SEC filing presence).

aiTitle: Add SBC fallback from EDGAR XBRL for yfinance nulls

content.text:
ABA-75 is shipped and **Done**.

## Summary

Added `get_sbc(ticker, periods=4)` to the EDGAR MCP and wired it into `/stock-signal` as the fallback for when yfinance returns null SBC.

**The core insight** (verified live before designing): KSPI and other 20-F foreign filers report XBRL facts under the `ifrs-full` namespace in local currency, not `us-gaap`/USD — so the tool searches both namespaces (`us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensation` → `ifrs-full:ExpenseFromSharebasedPaymentTransactionsWithEmployees`), handles non-USD units, and includes 20-F forms.

**A gap I found and closed:** the EDGAR server existed in code (built under ABA-13/14) but was never registered in `.mcp.json` — only `yf` was. Without registering it, the `/signal` fallback would have been inert, so I added it.

**Done-when, all verified live:**
- `get_sbc("KSPI", 4)` → 4 years from the 20-F XBRL facts in KZT ✅
- US 10-K sanity check → NVDA / CRM / AAPL in USD ✅
- `/signal` calls it automatically and tags `source: edgar` ✅

**Review:** 5-axis sub-agent review surfaced 1 Critical + 3 Required + 3 Nice-to-have. The Critical was a genuine landmine — the FX-conversion direction in the skill was unspecified, risking an SBC figure wrong by ~5 orders of magnitude (KZT↔USD). Fixed with explicit multiply-direction + a magnitude sanity-check. All Required findings addressed (deterministic currency selection, documented + tested us-gaap-first precedence, semantic-proxy caveat). 46 tests pass (was 29).

Committed `ebdf755`, pushed to main, Linear comment posted, issue moved to Done.

One thing worth flagging: the EDGAR server registration only takes effect for **future** Claude Code sessions (MCP servers connect at startup), so `/signal` can't actually invoke `get_sbc` until this session's successor — but the wiring and the tool itself are verified correct.
